_provenance.source: Du (2026), 'When Facts Aren't Payoffs: The Resolution-Settlement Gap in Prediction Markets', Table A3 (Appendix B.3)
_provenance.construction: Buckets built from keyword matches on the question text. Polymarket's own category tags are noisy; the paper uses these text-classified buckets for the chain-type x category cross-tab.
_provenance.sample: Binary-binary disputed subset (N=2,117). Within-row gaps between N_all and N_C + N_D (12 contracts in the 'All' row) reflect single-episode disputed contracts whose dispute event is parsed without a separate proposal episode and sit outside the Path C/Path D split.

interpretation: Within the four cleanly-identifiable buckets, Path D revision rates exceed Path C rates in Sports (49.2 vs 16.1) and Crypto (38.0 vs 6.2). Politics/Geopolitics reverses the pattern: Path C revisions (29.5%) outnumber Path D (14.4%), consistent with high-salience political and geopolitical contracts being routed through DVM voiding rather than parallel re-resolution. Weather/Nature has the lowest overall revision rate (6.0%) and zero Path D revisions out of 10, consistent with auto-resolving event types where the underlying datum is unambiguous.
